claim_boundary: On one frozen public dependency program, normalized exact temporal credit selected a finite intervention placement that beat its node-tied projection and every locked timing permutation.
This is a contaminated network-zero mechanism result compiled from the known v0.5.3 factorized-lineage engineering repair; it is not fresh reasoning or generalization evidence.
The committed v0.5.3 source, graph, closure, grade, and regression fingerprints are immutable inputs and are neither rerun nor regraded by v0.5.4.

Fixtures select symbolic schedule, event, terminal, and control policies only; numeric transition algebra, Jacobians, actuator normalization, controls, and terminal targets are mechanically compiled from pinned source code and the sealed public lineage program.

Gradients and exact adjoints exist only inside the public local recurrence. No gradient crosses a semantic adapter, JSON boundary, provider API, model sampling process, or generated natural-language output.
Early and late lanes are two schedules over the same supplied public program, not independent benchmark replications or independent reasoning agents.
The stable-constraint lane is a disconnected no-event identity witness with constraint-only terminal axes, zero controls, zero backward calls, and neutral output equal to controlled output.

Passing the 17 hard gates permits only the stated local temporal-placement claim and v0.5.5 substrate work; it does not establish hidden-state editing, hosted-model improvement, semantic discovery, causal superiority in natural language, production readiness, or benchmark generality.
Any failed hard gate freezes STOP_V0_5_5_TEMPORAL_GATE under this namespace; no failed comparison may be tuned, relabeled, or rescued by opening v0.5.5.
